subject: Welcome to Sober Helpline — start here
preview: Your first step: calm the chaos and focus on what matters most.
headline: Start Here
intro: Welcome to Sober Helpline. You do not need to solve everything today.
If addiction is affecting your family, the first goal is not perfection — it is stabilization. This track will help you slow things down, stop reacting blindly, and focus on the next right step.
forumPrompt: What feels most urgent in your family right now?

subject: The boundary problem almost every family faces
preview: Clear boundaries are not cruelty. They are structure you can actually hold.
headline: Boundaries
intro: Most families know they need boundaries long before they know how to hold them.
Today's track will help you separate healthy limits from fear, anger, rescuing, and emotional ultimatums.
forumPrompt: What boundary do you know you need to set, but haven't yet?

subject: Before you say yes to treatment, read this
preview: Families deserve better than guessing and getting sold.
headline: Treatment Decisions
intro: Treatment decisions are hard enough without mixed messages, pressure, and marketing.
This track will help you ask better questions, spot red flags, and think more clearly about what kind of help is actually needed.
forumPrompt: What confuses you most about treatment options right now?

subject: If relapse happens, do this next
preview: Relapse is painful, but chaos does not have to take over again.
headline: Relapse Response
intro: Relapse can trigger panic, rage, and deep disappointment in a family system.
This track is about responding more strategically so you are not just repeating the same cycle again.
forumPrompt: How has your family usually responded after relapse?

subject: The painful patterns that keep families stuck
preview: Sometimes the family adapts around addiction without realizing it.
headline: Family Patterns
intro: Addiction does not just affect one person. It reshapes the whole family system.
This track will help you see the hidden roles, loyalty conflicts, and enabling patterns that can keep everyone stuck.
forumPrompt: What pattern keeps repeating in your family no matter how hard everyone tries?

subject: Your life cannot stay on hold forever
preview: Your recovery matters too — even if your loved one is still struggling.
headline: Your Recovery Matters Too
intro: Families often put their own lives on hold while they wait for someone else to change.
Today's track is about reclaiming your footing, your values, and your peace — even in the middle of uncertainty.
forumPrompt: What part of your own life has addiction pushed to the side?

subject: Choose the track that fits your family best
preview: Now choose the education path that fits your family's stage.
headline: Choose Your Path
intro: You have seen the shape of the library this week. Now it is time to choose the track that fits your family best.
You may need crisis help, boundaries, treatment guidance, relapse support, or a deeper look at family patterns. Start where the pressure is highest.
forumPrompt: Which track fits your situation best right now: crisis, boundaries, treatment, relapse, or family patterns?
